notes: Process assertions (3 new): with_skill 3/3 pass, without_skill 0/3 pass — skill consistently runs divergence check, branch collision check, and --base flag; baseline skips all three.
Output quality assertions (5 original): both configurations 5/5 — Claude naturally produces conventional commits, proper branch prefixes, and structured PR bodies regardless of skill guidance.
The skill adds ~41s and ~5000 tokens overhead per run from reading the skill file and following the structured checklist.
Timing caveat: without_skill is faster partly because it skips the 3 process checks the skill mandates.
All 3 failed assertions in without_skill are omissions (things not done), not errors — Claude doesn't actively do the wrong thing, it just skips safety steps.
